concerns.text: Task T027 implements 'Mean Signed Correlation (MSC)' as the primary metric, overriding FR-005 and Constitution Principle VII which explicitly mandate 'Mean Absolute Correlation (MAC)'. This is a semantic weakening of the core research variable definition without a spec amendment.

Task T034 states 'apply FDR correction to the global mean metric if tested'. FR-008 and FR-011 strictly limit FDR correction to 'edge-wise' testing (multiple edges). Applying it to a single global metric is a misinterpretation that dilutes the multiple-comparison constraint.

Task T036 defines the sensitivity curve as 'p-values vs motion thresholds'. SC-005 requires the curve to measure 'the proportion of subjects retained' (retention rate) across thresholds. The task implements a different metric, failing to satisfy the acceptance criterion.

Task T023 introduces 'nuisance regression (motion params + derivatives, WM, CSF, global signal)'. The spec (FR-001 to FR-005) does not authorize global signal regression or specific nuisance regressors. This adds an unverified processing step that alters the data pipeline beyond the spec's scope.

Task T035 only stores the 'standard deviation of the permutation null distribution'. SC-002 requires measuring the 'stability of the connectivity strength metric' *against* this value. The task lacks the verification/comparison step required to satisfy the success criterion.

Task T037 enforces 'associational framing' but does not mandate the literal string 'associational' in the output. FR-007 and SC-003 explicitly require the output report to contain the literal word 'associational' if randomization is unverified. The task is too vague to guarantee compliance.
